summary: Import-time credential exfiltration and reverse shell to hardcoded IP
details: On require() of the package's main entry (lib/index.js), a setTimeout-scheduled block executes two attacker payloads. (1) Credential exfiltration: scrapes process.env for keys matching /KEY|SECR|TOK|PASS|PRIV|MNEM|AWS|FIRE|DB_|PG|SIGN|VAULT|SEED|DEPLOY/i, executes `id` and `whoami`, reads `/proc/1/environ`, and POSTs the resulting JSON (hostname, username, matching env vars, cwd, id output, container init env) to 2.25.186.116:8443/t. (2) Reverse shell: opens a TCP socket to 2.25.186.116:4444, spawns `/bin/sh -i`, and bidirectionally pipes stdio across the socket; on socket error falls back to a python3 socket+dup2+subprocess reverse shell. All sensitive strings (IP, `/bin/sh`, `python3`, `http`, `child_process`, `net`) are obfuscated via String.fromCharCode arrays to evade pattern scanners. The package advertises itself as 'shared UI constants and utilities' but ships only a handful of trivial constants alongside this payload, indicating the stated purpose is a cover for the backdoor. Any consumer that imports this module on a build server, CI runner, or developer workstation leaks credentials and grants the attacker an interactive shell on the host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
